content: I'm trying to decide what new skill to learn next. I already do drone photography and home brewing, but I want to challenge myself with something different.

That's exciting! With your background in drone photography and brewing, you clearly enjoy both technical and creative pursuits. Have you considered something that combines those elements, or are you looking for something completely different?
Actually, I've been really into speedcubing lately. I can solve a 3x3 in about 45 seconds now, but I want to get faster. I'm thinking of starting a YouTube channel to document my progress.

Speedcubing is a fantastic choice! A YouTube channel documenting your journey from 45 seconds to sub-20 could really resonate with viewers. Since you're already comfortable with drone photography, you could create some really engaging tutorial videos.

That's exactly what I was thinking. I'm 34 and work as a cybersecurity consultant, so I have some technical skills that might help with the channel setup. But I'm not sure where to start with content strategy.

Your cybersecurity background could actually be a unique angle - maybe you could explore the algorithms and mathematics behind speedcubing from a technical perspective. Have you thought about what your first few videos would cover?

I was thinking of starting with my current solving method and then documenting each new technique I learn. Maybe I could also review different cubes and mods, since I've tried quite a few different ones.

That's a solid content plan! Your experience with different cubes would definitely add value for viewers. Since you mentioned home brewing, have you considered doing any creative crossover content, like solving a cube while brewing or something fun like that?

Actually, that's not a bad idea! I could do a 'brew and solve' series where I time myself solving different cubes while waiting for various brewing stages. It would combine two of my hobbies in a unique way.

That sounds like a really engaging series concept! Your viewers would get to see both your brewing process and speedcubing progress. Have you decided on a channel name yet, or are you still brainstorming?
I've been playing with names like 'Cube & Craft' or 'Pixel Puzzles & Pints.' I want something that reflects both my tech background and my hands-on hobbies.

Both of those are great options! 'Cube & Craft' has a nice ring to it and clearly communicates what viewers can expect. Since you work in cybersecurity, have you thought about how you'll protect your channel and personal information online?

Good point. I was planning to use a separate email and maybe even a PO box for business inquiries. I've dealt with enough security breaches in my work to know I need to be careful about my digital footprint.

That's very wise. Setting up proper security from the start is crucial. Since you mentioned your cybersecurity work, do you think you might incorporate any tech/security related content into your channel, or do you prefer to keep those worlds separate?

I think I'll keep them mostly separate, but maybe I could do some educational content about the math behind encryption and how it relates to speedcubing algorithms. That could be an interesting niche angle.

That's a fascinating crossover idea! You could really carve out a unique space in the speedcubing community. Have you started looking into the equipment you'll need for filming your solves and tutorials?

I've got most of what I need from my drone photography setup - good camera, tripod, lighting. I might need to invest in a better microphone though. I want the audio to be crystal clear for tutorial videos.
